22	
23	/*
24	 * build-registry.mts is the single v4 registry pipeline.
25	 *
26	 * Source of truth:
27	 * - Authored raw component/registry source lives in registry/bases/base and
28	 *   registry/bases/radix.
29	 * - Authored demo source lives in examples/base and examples/radix.
30	 * - Style tokens live in registry/styles/style-*.css.
31	 *
32	 * Persistent outputs:
33	 * - registry/bases/__index__.tsx
34	 * - registry/__index__.tsx
35	 * - examples/__index__.tsx
36	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui/*
37	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui-rtl/* for base-nova and radix-nova only
38	 * - public/r/*
39	 *
40	 * Temporary outputs:
41	 * - registry/<base-style>/*
42	 * - registry-<style>.json
43	 *
44	 * Execution order:
45	 * 1. Build registry/bases/__index__.tsx from the authored base registries.
46	 * 2. Build temporary styled registries under registry/<base-style>.
47	 * 3. Build registry/__index__.tsx for runtime lookup across legacy styles and
48	 *    generated base-style combinations.
49	 * 4. Build examples/__index__.tsx from authored demos.
50	 * 5. Export public/r/* for every style through the shadcn CLI.
51	 * 6. Copy compiled ui/* from the temporary registries into styles/<style>/ui.
52	 * 7. Build styles/<style>/ui-rtl for base-nova and radix-nova only.
53	 * 8. Format the generated persistent outputs.
54	 * 9. Clean up the temporary registry/<base-style> trees and registry-*.json.
55	 */
56	
57	const STYLE_COMBINATIONS = Array.from(BASES).flatMap((base) =>
58	  STYLES.map((style) => ({

61	    name: `${base.name}-${style.name}`,
62	    title: `${base.title} ${style.title}`,
63	  }))
64	)

66	const CPU_COUNT = availableParallelism()

68	const FILE_BUILD_CONCURRENCY = Math.max(4, Math.min(CPU_COUNT, 8))
69	const COPY_CONCURRENCY = Math.max(4, Math.min(CPU_COUNT, 8))

94	  const stylesToBuild = new Map<string, { name: string; title: string }>()

96	  for (const style of legacyStyles) {

104	    })

4	import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
5	
6	import { cn } from "@/registry/bases/base/lib/utils"
7	
8	const buttonVariants = cva(
9	  "cn-button group/button inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none select-none disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0",
10	  {
11	    variants: {
12	      variant: {
13	        default: "cn-button-variant-default",
14	        outline: "cn-button-variant-outline",
15	        secondary: "cn-button-variant-secondary",
16	        ghost: "cn-button-variant-ghost",
17	        destructive: "cn-button-variant-destructive",
18	        link: "cn-button-variant-link",
19	      },
20	      size: {
21	        default: "cn-button-size-default",
22	        xs: "cn-button-size-xs",
23	        sm: "cn-button-size-sm",
24	        lg: "cn-button-size-lg",
25	        icon: "cn-button-size-icon",
26	        "icon-xs": "cn-button-size-icon-xs",
27	        "icon-sm": "cn-button-size-icon-sm",
28	        "icon-lg": "cn-button-size-icon-lg",
29	      },
30	    },
31	    defaultVariants: {
32	      variant: "default",
33	      size: "default",
34	    },
35	  }
36	)
37	
38	function Button({
39	  className,
40	  variant = "default",
41	  size = "default",
42	  ...props
43	}: ButtonPrimitive.Props & VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants>) {
44	  return (
45	    <ButtonPrimitive
46	      data-slot="button"
47	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
48	      {...props}
49	    />
50	  )
51	}
52	
53	export { Button, buttonVariants }

44	              identify growth opportunities.
45	            </CardDescription>
46	          </CardHeader>
47	          <CardContent className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">

51	      </TabsContent>

60	          </CardHeader>

1 --- 2 title: registry-item.json 3 description: Specification for registry items. 4 --- 5 6 The `registry-item.json` schema is used to define your custom registry items. 7 8 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 9 { 10 "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json", 11 "name": "hello-world", 12 "type": "registry:block", 13 "title": "Hello World", 14 "description": "A simple hello world component.", 15 "registryDependencies": [ 16 "button", 17 "@acme/input-form", 18 "https://example.com/r/foo" 19 ], 20 "dependencies": ["is-even@3.0.0", "motion"], 21 "devDependencies": ["tw-animate-css"], 22 "files": [ 23 { 24 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/hello-world.tsx", 25 "type": "registry:component" 26 }, 27 { 28 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/use-hello-world.ts", 29 "type": "registry:hook" 30 } 31 ], 32 "cssVars": { 33 "theme": { 34 "font-heading": "Poppins, sans-serif" 35 }, 36 "light": { 37 "brand": "oklch(0.205 0.015 18)" 38 }, 39 "dark": { 40 "brand": "oklch(0.205 0.015 18)" 41 } 42 } 43 } 44 ``` 45 46
47 See more examples 48
49 50 ## Definitions 51 52 You can see the JSON Schema for `registry-item.json` [here](https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json). 53 54 ### $schema 55 56 The `$schema` property is used to specify the schema for the `registry-item.json` file. 57 58 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 59 { 60 "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json" 61 } 62 ``` 63 64 ### name 65 66 The name of the item. This is used to identify the item in the registry. It should be unique for your registry. 67 68 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 69 { 70 "name": "hello-world" 71 } 72 ``` 73 74 ### title 75 76 A human-readable title for your registry item. Keep it short and descriptive. 77 78 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 79 { 80 "title": "Hello World" 81 } 82 ``` 83 84 ### description 85 86 A description of your registry item. This can be longer and more detailed than the `title`. 87 88 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 89 { 90 "description": "A simple hello world component." 91 } 92 ``` 93 94 ### type 95 96 The `type` property is used to specify the type of your registry item. This is used to determine the type and target path of the item when resolved for a project. 97 98 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 99 { 100 "type": "registry:block" 101 } 102 ``` 103 104 The following types are supported: 105 106 | Type | Description | 107 | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | 108 | `registry:base` | Use for entire design systems. | 109 | `registry:block` | Use for complex components with multiple files. | 110 | `registry:component` | Use for simple components. | 111 | `registry:font` | Use for fonts. | 112 | `registry:lib` | Use for lib and utils. | 113 | `registry:hook` | Use for hooks. | 114 | `registry:ui` | Use for UI components and single-file primitives. | 115 | `registry:page` | Use for page or file-based routes. | 116 | `registry:file` | Use for miscellaneous files. | 117 | `registry:style` | Use for registry styles. eg. `new-york`. | 118 | `registry:theme` | Use for themes. | 119 | `registry:item` | Use for universal registry items. | 120 121 ### author 122 123 The `author` property is used to specify the author of the registry item. 124 125 It can be unique to the registry item or the same as the author of the registry. 126 127 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 128 { 129 "author": "John Doe " 130 } 131 ``` 132 133 ### dependencies 134 135 The `dependencies` property is used to specify the dependencies of your registry item. This is for `npm` packages. 136 137 Use `@version` to specify the version of your registry item. 138 139 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 140 { 141 "dependencies": [ 142 "@radix-ui/react-accordion", 143 "zod", 144 "lucide-react", 145 "name@1.0.2" 146 ] 147 } 148 ``` 149 150 ### devDependencies 151 152 The `devDependencies` property is used to specify the dev dependencies of your registry item. These are `npm` packages that are only needed during development. 153 154 Use `@version` to specify the version of the package. 155 156 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 157 { 158 "devDependencies": ["tw-animate-css", "name@1.2.0"] 159 } 160 ``` 161 162 ### registryDependencies 163 164 Used for registry dependencies. Can be names, namespaced or URLs. 165 166 - For `shadcn/ui` registry items such as `button`, `input`, `select`, etc use the name eg. `['button', 'input', 'select']`. 167 - For namespaced registry items such as `@acme` use the name eg. `['@acme/input-form']`. 168 - For custom registry items use the URL of the registry item eg. `['https://example.com/r/hello-world.json']`. 169 170 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 171 { 172 "registryDependencies": [ 173 "button", 174 "@acme/input-form", 175 "https://example.com/r/editor.json" 176 ] 177 } 178 ``` 179 180 Note: The CLI will automatically resolve remote registry dependencies. 181 182 ### files 183 184 The `files` property is used to specify the files of your registry item. Each file has a `path`, `type` and `target` (optional) property. 185 186 **The `target` property is required for `registry:page` and `registry:file` types.** 187 188 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 189 { 190 "files": [ 191 { 192 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/page.tsx", 193 "type": "registry:page", 194 "target": "app/hello/page.tsx" 195 }, 196 { 197 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/hello-world.tsx", 198 "type": "registry:component" 199 }, 200 { 201 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/use-hello-world.ts", 202 "type": "registry:hook" 203 }, 204 { 205 "path": "registry/new-york/hello-world/.env", 206 "type": "registry:file", 207 "target": "~/.env" 208 } 209 ] 210 } 211 ``` 212 213 #### path 214 215 The `path` property is used to specify the path to the file in your registry. This path is used by the build script to parse, transform and build the registry JSON payload. 216 217 #### type 218 219 The `type` property is used to specify the type of the file. See the [type](#type) section for more information. 220 221 #### target 222 223 The `target` property is used to indicate where the file should be placed in a project. This is optional and only required for `registry:page` and `registry:file` types. 224 225 By default, the `shadcn` cli will read a project's `components.json` file to determine the target path. For some files, such as routes or config you can specify the target path manually. 226 227 Use `~` to refer to the root of the project e.g `~/foo.config.js`. 228 229 ### tailwind 230 231 **DEPRECATED:** Use `cssVars.theme` instead for Tailwind v4 projects. 232 233 The `tailwind` property is used for tailwind configuration such as `theme`, `plugins` and `content`. 234 235 You can use the `tailwind.config` property to add colors, animations and plugins to your registry item. 236 237 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 238 { 239 "tailwind": { 240 "config": { 241 "theme": { 242 "extend": { 243 "colors": { 244 "brand": "hsl(var(--brand))" 245 }, 246 "keyframes": { 247 "wiggle": { 248 "0%, 100%": { "transform": "rotate(-3deg)" }, 249 "50%": { "transform": "rotate(3deg)" } 250 } 251 }, 252 "animation": { 253 "wiggle": "wiggle 1s ease-in-out infinite" 254 } 255 } 256 } 257 } 258 } 259 } 260 ``` 261 262 ### cssVars 263 264 Use to define CSS variables for your registry item. 265 266 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 267 { 268 "cssVars": { 269 "theme": { 270 "font-heading": "Poppins, sans-serif" 271 }, 272 "light": { 273 "brand": "20 14.3% 4.1%", 274 "radius": "0.5rem" 275 }, 276 "dark": { 277 "brand": "20 14.3% 4.1%" 278 } 279 } 280 } 281 ``` 282 283 ### css 284 285 Use `css` to add new rules to the project's CSS file eg. `@layer base`, `@layer components`, `@utility`, `@keyframes`, `@plugin`, etc. 286 287 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 288 { 289 "css": { 290 "@plugin @tailwindcss/typography": {}, 291 "@plugin foo": {}, 292 "@layer base": { 293 "body": { 294 "font-size": "var(--text-base)", 295 "line-height": "1.5" 296 } 297 }, 298 "@layer components": { 299 "button": { 300 "background-color": "var(--color-primary)", 301 "color": "var(--color-white)" 302 } 303 }, 304 "@utility text-magic": { 305 "font-size": "var(--text-base)", 306 "line-height": "1.5" 307 }, 308 "@keyframes wiggle": { 309 "0%, 100%": { 310 "transform": "rotate(-3deg)" 311 }, 312 "50%": { 313 "transform": "rotate(3deg)" 314 } 315 } 316 } 317 } 318 ``` 319 320 ### envVars 321 322 Use `envVars` to add environment variables to your registry item. 323 324 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 325 { 326 "envVars": { 327 "NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL": "http://localhost:4000", 328 "DATABASE_URL": "postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/postgres", 329 "OPENAI_API_KEY": "" 330 } 331 } 332 ``` 333 334 Environment variables are added to the `.env.local` or `.env` file. Existing variables are not overwritten. 335 336 337 338 **IMPORTANT:** Use `envVars` to add development or example variables. Do NOT use it to add production variables. 339 340 341 342 ### font 343 344 The `font` property is required for `registry:font` items. It configures the font family, provider, import name, CSS variable, and the npm package to install for non-Next.js projects. 345 346 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 347 { 348 "font": { 349 "family": "'Inter Variable', sans-serif", 350 "provider": "google", 351 "import": "Inter", 352 "variable": "--font-sans", 353 "subsets": ["latin"], 354 "dependency": "@fontsource-variable/inter" 355 } 356 } 357 ``` 358 359 | Property | Type | Required | Description | 360 | ------------ | ---------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 361 | `family` | `string` | Yes | The CSS font-family value. | 362 | `provider` | `string` | Yes | The font provider. Currently only `google` is supported. | 363 | `import` | `string` | Yes | The import name for the font from `next/font/google`. | 364 | `variable` | `string` | Yes | The CSS variable name for the font (e.g., `--font-sans`, `--font-mono`). | 365 | `weight` | `string[]` | No | Array of font weights to include. | 366 | `subsets` | `string[]` | No | Array of font subsets to include. | 367 | `selector` | `string` | No | CSS selector to apply the font to. Defaults to `html`. | 368 | `dependency` | `string` | No | The npm package to install for non-Next.js projects (e.g., `@fontsource-variable/inter`). | 369 370 ### docs 371 372 Use `docs` to show custom documentation or message when installing your registry item via the CLI. 373 374 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 375 { 376 "docs": "To get an OPENAI_API_KEY, sign up for an account at https://platform.openai.com." 377 } 378 ``` 379 380 ### categories 381 382 Use `categories` to organize your registry item. 383 384 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 385 { 386 "categories": ["sidebar", "dashboard"] 387 } 388 ``` 389 390 ### meta 391 392 Use `meta` to add additional metadata to your registry item. You can add any key/value pair that you want to be available to the registry item. 393 394 ```json title="registry-item.json" showLineNumbers 395 { 396 "meta": { "foo": "bar" } 397 } 398 ``` 399

1	---
2	title: Examples
3	description: "Examples of registry items: styles, components, css vars, etc."
4	---
5	
6	## registry:style
7	
8	### Custom style that extends shadcn/ui
9	
10	The following registry item is a custom style that extends shadcn/ui. On `npx shadcn init`, it will:
11	
12	- Install `@tabler/icons-react` as a dependency.
13	- Add the `login-01` block and `calendar` component to the project.
14	- Add the `editor` from a remote registry.
15	- Set the `font-sans` variable to `Inter, sans-serif`.
16	- Install a `brand` color in light and dark mode.
17	
18	```json title="example-style.json" showLineNumbers
19	{
20	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
21	  "name": "example-style",
22	  "type": "registry:style",
23	  "dependencies": ["@tabler/icons-react"],
24	  "registryDependencies": [
25	    "login-01",
26	    "calendar",
27	    "https://example.com/r/editor.json"
28	  ],
29	  "cssVars": {
30	    "theme": {
31	      "font-sans": "Inter, sans-serif"
32	    },
33	    "light": {
34	      "brand": "20 14.3% 4.1%"
35	    },
36	    "dark": {
37	      "brand": "20 14.3% 4.1%"
38	    }
39	  }
40	}
41	```
42	
43	### Custom style from scratch
44	
45	The following registry item is a custom style that doesn't extend shadcn/ui. See the `extends: none` field.
46	
47	It can be used to create a new style from scratch, i.e. custom components, css vars, dependencies, etc.
48	
49	On `npx shadcn add`, the following will:
50	
51	- Install `tailwind-merge` and `clsx` as dependencies.
52	- Add the `utils` registry item from the shadcn/ui registry.
53	- Add the `button`, `input`, `label`, and `select` components from a remote registry.
54	- Install new css vars: `main`, `bg`, `border`, `text`, `ring`.
55	
56	```json title="example-style.json" showLineNumbers
57	{
58	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
59	  "extends": "none",
60	  "name": "new-style",
61	  "type": "registry:style",
62	  "dependencies": ["tailwind-merge", "clsx"],
63	  "registryDependencies": [
64	    "utils",
65	    "https://example.com/r/button.json",
66	    "https://example.com/r/input.json",
67	    "https://example.com/r/label.json",
68	    "https://example.com/r/select.json"
69	  ],
70	  "cssVars": {
71	    "theme": {
72	      "font-sans": "Inter, sans-serif"
73	    },
74	    "light": {
75	      "main": "#88aaee",
76	      "bg": "#dfe5f2",
77	      "border": "#000",
78	      "text": "#000",
79	      "ring": "#000"
80	    },
81	    "dark": {
82	      "main": "#88aaee",
83	      "bg": "#272933",
84	      "border": "#000",
85	      "text": "#e6e6e6",
86	      "ring": "#fff"
87	    }
88	  }
89	}
90	```
91	
92	## registry:theme

102	    "light": {
103	      "background": "oklch(1 0 0)",
104	      "foreground": "oklch(0.141 0.005 285.823)",
105	      "primary": "oklch(0.546 0.245 262.881)",
106	      "primary-foreground": "oklch(0.97 0.014 254.604)",
107	      "ring": "oklch(0.746 0.16 232.661)",
108	      "sidebar-primary": "oklch(0.546 0.245 262.881)",
109	      "sidebar-primary-foreground": "oklch(0.97 0.014 254.604)",
110	      "sidebar-ring": "oklch(0.746 0.16 232.661)"
111	    },
112	    "dark": {
113	      "background": "oklch(1 0 0)",
114	      "foreground": "oklch(0.141 0.005 285.823)",
115	      "primary": "oklch(0.707 0.165 254.624)",
116	      "primary-foreground": "oklch(0.97 0.014 254.604)",
117	      "ring": "oklch(0.707 0.165 254.624)",
118	      "sidebar-primary": "oklch(0.707 0.165 254.624)",
119	      "sidebar-primary-foreground": "oklch(0.97 0.014 254.604)",

126	### Custom colors
127	
128	The following style will init using shadcn/ui defaults and then add a custom `brand` color.
129

132	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",

134	  "type": "registry:style",

138	    },

140	      "brand": "oklch(0.14 0.00 286)"
141	    }
142	  }

145	
146	## registry:block
147	
148	### Custom block
149	
150	This block installs the `login-01` block from the shadcn/ui registry.
151	
152	```json title="login-01.json" showLineNumbers
153	{
154	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
155	  "name": "login-01",
156	  "type": "registry:block",
157	  "description": "A simple login form.",
158	  "registryDependencies": ["button", "card", "input", "label"],
159	  "files": [
160	    {
161	      "path": "blocks/login-01/page.tsx",
162	      "content": "import { LoginForm } ...",
163	      "type": "registry:page",
164	      "target": "app/login/page.tsx"
165	    },
166	    {
167	      "path": "blocks/login-01/components/login-form.tsx",
168	      "content": "...",
169	      "type": "registry:component"
170	    }
171	  ]
172	}
173	```
174	
175	### Install a block and override primitives
176	
177	You can install a block from the shadcn/ui registry and override the primitives using your custom ones.
178	
179	On `npx shadcn add`, the following will:
180	
181	- Add the `login-01` block from the shadcn/ui registry.
182	- Override the `button`, `input`, and `label` primitives with the ones from the remote registry.
183	
184	```json title="example-style.json" showLineNumbers
185	{
186	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
187	  "name": "custom-login",
188	  "type": "registry:block",
189	  "registryDependencies": [
190	    "login-01",
191	    "https://example.com/r/button.json",
192	    "https://example.com/r/input.json",
193	    "https://example.com/r/label.json"
194	  ]
195	}
196	```
197	
198	## registry:ui
199	
200	### UI component
201	
202	A `registry:ui` item is a reusable UI component. It can have dependencies, registry dependencies, and CSS variables.
203	
204	```json title="button.json" showLineNumbers
205	{
206	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
207	  "name": "button",
208	  "type": "registry:ui",
209	  "dependencies": ["radix-ui"],
210	  "files": [
211	    {
212	      "path": "ui/button.tsx",
213	      "content": "...",
214	      "type": "registry:ui"
215	    }
216	  ]
217	}
218	```
219	
220	### UI component with CSS variables
221	
222	```json title="sidebar.json" showLineNumbers
223	{
224	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
225	  "name": "sidebar",
226	  "type": "registry:ui",
227	  "dependencies": ["radix-ui"],
228	  "registryDependencies": ["button", "separator", "sheet", "tooltip"],
229	  "files": [
230	    {
231	      "path": "ui/sidebar.tsx",
232	      "content": "...",
233	      "type": "registry:ui"
234	    }
235	  ],
236	  "cssVars": {
237	    "light": {
238	      "sidebar-background": "oklch(0.985 0 0)",
239	      "sidebar-foreground": "oklch(0.141 0.005 285.823)",
240	      "sidebar-border": "oklch(0.92 0.004 286.32)"
241	    },
242	    "dark": {
243	      "sidebar-background": "oklch(0.141 0.005 285.823)",
244	      "sidebar-foreground": "oklch(0.985 0 0)",
245	      "sidebar-border": "oklch(0.274 0.006 286.033)"
246	    }
247	  }
248	}
249	```
250	
251	## registry:lib
252	
253	### Utility library
254	
255	A `registry:lib` item is a utility library. Use it to share helper functions, constants, or other non-component code.
256	
257	```json title="utils.json" showLineNumbers
258	{
259	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
260	  "name": "utils",
261	  "type": "registry:lib",
262	  "dependencies": ["clsx", "tailwind-merge"],
263	  "files": [
264	    {
265	      "path": "lib/utils.ts",
266	      "content": "import { clsx, type ClassValue } from \"clsx\"\nimport { twMerge } from \"tailwind-merge\"\n\nexport function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {\n  return twMerge(clsx(inputs))\n}",
267	      "type": "registry:lib"
268	    }
269	  ]
270	}
271	```
272	
273	## registry:hook
274	
275	### Custom hook
276	
277	A `registry:hook` item is a custom React hook.
278	
279	```json title="use-mobile.json" showLineNumbers
280	{
281	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
282	  "name": "use-mobile",
283	  "type": "registry:hook",
284	  "files": [
285	    {
286	      "path": "hooks/use-mobile.ts",
287	      "content": "...",
288	      "type": "registry:hook"
289	    }
290	  ]
291	}
292	```
293	
294	### Hook with dependencies
295	
296	```json title="use-debounce.json" showLineNumbers
297	{
298	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
299	  "name": "use-debounce",
300	  "type": "registry:hook",
301	  "dependencies": ["react"],
302	  "files": [
303	    {
304	      "path": "hooks/use-debounce.ts",
305	      "content": "...",
306	      "type": "registry:hook"
307	    }
308	  ]
309	}
310	```
311	
312	## registry:font
313	
314	### Custom font
315	
316	A `registry:font` item installs a Google Font. The `font` field is required and configures the font family, provider, import name, and CSS variable.
317	
318	```json title="font-inter.json" showLineNumbers
319	{
320	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
321	  "name": "font-inter",
322	  "type": "registry:font",
323	  "font": {
324	    "family": "'Inter Variable', sans-serif",
325	    "provider": "google",
326	    "import": "Inter",
327	    "variable": "--font-sans",
328	    "subsets": ["latin"],
329	    "dependency": "@fontsource-variable/inter"
330	  }
331	}
332	```
333	
334	### Monospace font
335	
336	```json title="font-jetbrains-mono.json" showLineNumbers
337	{
338	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
339	  "name": "font-jetbrains-mono",
340	  "type": "registry:font",
341	  "font": {
342	    "family": "'JetBrains Mono Variable', monospace",
343	    "provider": "google",
344	    "import": "JetBrains_Mono",
345	    "variable": "--font-mono",
346	    "weight": ["400", "500", "600", "700"],
347	    "subsets": ["latin"],
348	    "dependency": "@fontsource-variable/jetbrains-mono"
349	  }
350	}
351	```
352	
353	### Serif font
354	
355	```json title="font-lora.json" showLineNumbers
356	{
357	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
358	  "name": "font-lora",
359	  "type": "registry:font",
360	  "font": {
361	    "family": "'Lora Variable', serif",
362	    "provider": "google",
363	    "import": "Lora",
364	    "variable": "--font-serif",
365	    "subsets": ["latin"],
366	    "dependency": "@fontsource-variable/lora"
367	  }
368	}
369	```
370	
371	### Font with custom selector
372	
373	Use the `selector` field to apply a font to specific CSS selectors instead of globally on `html`. This is useful for heading fonts or other targeted font applications.
374	
375	```json title="font-playfair-display.json" showLineNumbers
376	{
377	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
378	  "name": "font-playfair-display",
379	  "type": "registry:font",
380	  "font": {
381	    "family": "'Playfair Display Variable', serif",
382	    "provider": "google",
383	    "import": "Playfair_Display",
384	    "variable": "--font-heading",
385	    "subsets": ["latin"],
386	    "selector": "h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6",
387	    "dependency": "@fontsource-variable/playfair-display"
388	  }
389	}
390	```
391	
392	When `selector` is set, the font utility class (e.g. `font-heading`) is applied via CSS `@apply` on the specified selector within `@layer base`, instead of being added to the `<html>` element. The CSS variable is still injected on `<html>` so it's available globally.
393	
394	## registry:base
395	
396	### Custom base
397	
398	A `registry:base` item is a complete design system base. It defines the full set of dependencies, CSS variables, and configuration for a project. The `config` field is unique to `registry:base` items.
399	
400	The `config` field accepts the following properties (all optional):
401	
402	| Property             | Type                                                                         | Description                                                     |
403	| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
404	| `style`              | `string`                                                                     | The style name for the base.                                    |
405	| `iconLibrary`        | `string`                                                                     | The icon library to use (e.g. `lucide`).                        |
406	| `rsc`                | `boolean`                                                                    | Whether to enable React Server Components. Defaults to `false`. |
407	| `tsx`                | `boolean`                                                                    | Whether to use TypeScript. Defaults to `true`.                  |
408	| `rtl`                | `boolean`                                                                    | Whether to enable right-to-left support. Defaults to `false`.   |
409	| `menuColor`          | `"default" \| "inverted" \| "default-translucent" \| "inverted-translucent"` | The menu color scheme. Defaults to `"default"`.                 |
410	| `menuAccent`         | `"subtle" \| "bold"`                                                         | The menu accent style. Defaults to `"subtle"`.                  |
411	| `tailwind.baseColor` | `string`                                                                     | The base color name (e.g. `neutral`, `slate`, `zinc`).          |
412	| `tailwind.css`       | `string`                                                                     | Path to the Tailwind CSS file.                                  |
413	| `tailwind.prefix`    | `string`                                                                     | A prefix to add to all Tailwind classes.                        |
414	| `aliases.components` | `string`                                                                     | Import alias for components.                                    |
415	| `aliases.utils`      | `string`                                                                     | Import alias for utilities.                                     |
416	| `aliases.ui`         | `string`                                                                     | Import alias for UI components.                                 |
417	| `aliases.lib`        | `string`                                                                     | Import alias for lib.                                           |
418	| `aliases.hooks`      | `string`                                                                     | Import alias for hooks.                                         |
419	| `registries`         | `Record<string, string \| object>`                                           | Custom registry URLs. Keys must start with `@`.                 |
420	
421	```json title="custom-base.json" showLineNumbers
422	{
423	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
424	  "name": "custom-base",
425	  "type": "registry:base",
426	  "config": {
427	    "style": "custom-base",
428	    "iconLibrary": "lucide",
429	    "tailwind": {
430	      "baseColor": "neutral"
431	    }
432	  },
433	  "dependencies": [
434	    "class-variance-authority",
435	    "tw-animate-css",
436	    "lucide-react"
437	  ],
438	  "registryDependencies": ["utils", "font-inter"],
439	  "cssVars": {
440	    "light": {
441	      "background": "oklch(1 0 0)",
442	      "foreground": "oklch(0.141 0.005 285.823)",
443	      "primary": "oklch(0.21 0.006 285.885)",
444	      "primary-foreground": "oklch(0.985 0 0)"
445	    },
446	    "dark": {
447	      "background": "oklch(0.141 0.005 285.823)",
448	      "foreground": "oklch(0.985 0 0)",
449	      "primary": "oklch(0.985 0 0)",
450	      "primary-foreground": "oklch(0.21 0.006 285.885)"
451	    }
452	  },
453	  "css": {
454	    "@import \"tw-animate-css\"": {},
455	    "@layer base": {
456	      "*": {
457	        "@apply border-border outline-ring/50": {}
458	      },
459	      "body": {
460	        "@apply bg-background text-foreground": {}
461	      }
462	    }
463	  }
464	}
465	```
466	
467	### Base from scratch
468	
469	Use `extends: none` to create a base that doesn't extend shadcn/ui defaults.
470	
471	```json title="custom-base.json" showLineNumbers
472	{
473	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
474	  "name": "my-design-system",
475	  "extends": "none",
476	  "type": "registry:base",
477	  "config": {
478	    "style": "my-design-system",
479	    "iconLibrary": "lucide",
480	    "tailwind": {
481	      "baseColor": "slate"
482	    }
483	  },
484	  "dependencies": ["tailwind-merge", "clsx", "tw-animate-css", "lucide-react"],
485	  "registryDependencies": ["utils", "font-geist"],
486	  "cssVars": {
487	    "light": {
488	      "background": "oklch(1 0 0)",
489	      "foreground": "oklch(0.141 0.005 285.823)"
490	    },
491	    "dark": {
492	      "background": "oklch(0.141 0.005 285.823)",
493	      "foreground": "oklch(0.985 0 0)"
494	    }
495	  }
496	}
497	```
498	
499	## Common Fields
500	
501	### Author
502	
503	Use the `author` field to add attribution to your registry items.
504	
505	```json title="example-item.json" showLineNumbers {5}
506	{
507	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
508	  "name": "custom-component",
509	  "type": "registry:ui",
510	  "author": "shadcn",
511	  "files": [
512	    {
513	      "path": "ui/custom-component.tsx",
514	      "content": "...",
515	      "type": "registry:ui"
516	    }
517	  ]
518	}
519	```
520	
521	### Dev dependencies
522	
523	Use the `devDependencies` field to install packages as dev dependencies.
524	
525	```json title="example-item.json" showLineNumbers {5}
526	{
527	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
528	  "name": "custom-item",
529	  "type": "registry:item",
530	  "devDependencies": ["@types/mdx"],
531	  "files": [
532	    {
533	      "path": "lib/mdx.ts",
534	      "content": "...",
535	      "type": "registry:lib"
536	    }
537	  ]
538	}
539	```
540	
541	### Metadata
542	
543	Use the `meta` field to attach arbitrary metadata to your registry items. This can be used to store custom data that your tools or scripts can use.
544	
545	```json title="example-item.json" showLineNumbers {5-8}
546	{
547	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
548	  "name": "custom-component",
549	  "type": "registry:ui",
550	  "meta": {
551	    "category": "forms",
552	    "version": "2.0.0"
553	  },
554	  "files": [
555	    {
556	      "path": "ui/custom-component.tsx",
557	      "content": "...",
558	      "type": "registry:ui"
559	    }
560	  ]
561	}
562	```
563	
564	## CSS Variables
565	
566	### Custom Theme Variables
567	
568	Add custom theme variables to the `theme` object.
569	
570	```json title="example-theme.json" showLineNumbers
571	{
572	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
573	  "name": "custom-theme",
574	  "type": "registry:theme",
575	  "cssVars": {
576	    "theme": {
577	      "font-heading": "Inter, sans-serif",
578	      "shadow-card": "0 0 0 1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1)"
579	    }
580	  }
581	}
582	```
583	
584	### Override Tailwind CSS variables
585	
586	```json title="example-theme.json" showLineNumbers
587	{
588	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
589	  "name": "custom-theme",
590	  "type": "registry:theme",
591	  "cssVars": {
592	    "theme": {
593	      "spacing": "0.2rem",
594	      "breakpoint-sm": "640px",
595	      "breakpoint-md": "768px",
596	      "breakpoint-lg": "1024px",
597	      "breakpoint-xl": "1280px",
598	      "breakpoint-2xl": "1536px"
599	    }
600	  }
601	}
602	```
603	
604	## Add custom CSS
605	
606	### Base styles
607	
608	```json title="example-base.json" showLineNumbers
609	{
610	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
611	  "name": "custom-style",
612	  "type": "registry:style",
613	  "css": {
614	    "@layer base": {
615	      "h1": {
616	        "font-size": "var(--text-2xl)"
617	      },
618	      "h2": {
619	        "font-size": "var(--text-xl)"
620	      }
621	    }
622	  }
623	}
624	```
625	
626	### Components
627	
628	```json title="example-card.json" showLineNumbers
629	{
630	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
631	  "name": "custom-card",
632	  "type": "registry:component",
633	  "css": {
634	    "@layer components": {
635	      "card": {
636	        "background-color": "var(--color-white)",
637	        "border-radius": "var(--rounded-lg)",
638	        "padding": "var(--spacing-6)",
639	        "box-shadow": "var(--shadow-xl)"
640	      }
641	    }
642	  }
643	}
644	```
645	
646	## Add custom utilities
647	
648	### Simple utility
649	
650	```json title="example-component.json" showLineNumbers
651	{
652	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
653	  "name": "custom-component",
654	  "type": "registry:component",
655	  "css": {
656	    "@utility content-auto": {
657	      "content-visibility": "auto"
658	    }
659	  }
660	}
661	```
662	
663	### Complex utility
664	
665	```json title="example-utility.json" showLineNumbers
666	{
667	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
668	  "name": "custom-component",
669	  "type": "registry:component",
670	  "css": {
671	    "@utility scrollbar-hidden": {
672	      "scrollbar-hidden": {
673	        "&::-webkit-scrollbar": {
674	          "display": "none"
675	        }
676	      }
677	    }
678	  }
679	}
680	```
681	
682	### Functional utilities
683	
684	```json title="example-functional.json" showLineNumbers
685	{
686	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
687	  "name": "custom-component",
688	  "type": "registry:component",
689	  "css": {
690	    "@utility tab-*": {
691	      "tab-size": "var(--tab-size-*)"
692	    }
693	  }
694	}
695	```
696	
697	## Add CSS imports
698	
699	Use `@import` to add CSS imports to your registry item. The imports will be placed at the top of the CSS file.
700	
701	### Basic import
702	
703	```json title="example-import.json" showLineNumbers
704	{
705	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
706	  "name": "custom-import",
707	  "type": "registry:component",
708	  "css": {
709	    "@import \"tailwindcss\"": {},
710	    "@import \"./styles/base.css\"": {}
711	  }
712	}
713	```
714	
715	### Import with url() syntax
716	
717	```json title="example-url-import.json" showLineNumbers
718	{
719	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
720	  "name": "font-import",
721	  "type": "registry:item",
722	  "css": {
723	    "@import url(\"https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600&display=swap\")": {},
724	    "@import url('./local-styles.css')": {}
725	  }
726	}
727	```
728	
729	### Import with media queries
730	
731	```json title="example-media-import.json" showLineNumbers
732	{
733	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
734	  "name": "responsive-import",
735	  "type": "registry:item",
736	  "css": {
737	    "@import \"print-styles.css\" print": {},
738	    "@import url(\"mobile.css\") screen and (max-width: 768px)": {}
739	  }
740	}
741	```
742	
743	## Add custom plugins
744	
745	Use `@plugin` to add Tailwind plugins to your registry item. Plugins will be automatically placed after imports and before other content.
746	
747	**Important:** When using plugins from npm packages, you must also add them to the `dependencies` array.
748	
749	### Basic plugin usage
750	
751	```json title="example-plugin.json" showLineNumbers
752	{
753	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
754	  "name": "custom-plugin",
755	  "type": "registry:item",
756	  "css": {
757	    "@plugin \"@tailwindcss/typography\"": {},
758	    "@plugin \"foo\"": {}
759	  }
760	}
761	```
762	
763	### Plugin with npm dependency
764	
765	When using plugins from npm packages like `@tailwindcss/typography`, include them in the dependencies.
766	
767	```json title="example-typography.json" showLineNumbers
768	{
769	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
770	  "name": "typography-component",
771	  "type": "registry:item",
772	  "dependencies": ["@tailwindcss/typography"],
773	  "css": {
774	    "@plugin \"@tailwindcss/typography\"": {},
775	    "@layer components": {
776	      ".prose": {
777	        "max-width": "65ch"
778	      }
779	    }
780	  }
781	}
782	```
783	
784	### Scoped and file-based plugins
785	
786	```json title="example-scoped-plugin.json" showLineNumbers
787	{
788	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
789	  "name": "scoped-plugins",
790	  "type": "registry:component",
791	  "css": {
792	    "@plugin \"@headlessui/tailwindcss\"": {},
793	    "@plugin \"tailwindcss/plugin\"": {},
794	    "@plugin \"./custom-plugin.js\"": {}
795	  }
796	}
797	```
798	
799	### Multiple plugins with automatic ordering
800	
801	When you add multiple plugins, they are automatically grouped together and deduplicated.
802	
803	```json title="example-multiple-plugins.json" showLineNumbers
804	{
805	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
806	  "name": "multiple-plugins",
807	  "type": "registry:item",
808	  "dependencies": [
809	    "@tailwindcss/typography",
810	    "@tailwindcss/forms",
811	    "tw-animate-css"
812	  ],
813	  "css": {
814	    "@plugin \"@tailwindcss/typography\"": {},
815	    "@plugin \"@tailwindcss/forms\"": {},
816	    "@plugin \"tw-animate-css\"": {}
817	  }
818	}
819	```
820	
821	## Combined imports and plugins
822	
823	When using both `@import` and `@plugin` directives, imports are placed first, followed by plugins, then other CSS content.
824	
825	```json title="example-combined.json" showLineNumbers
826	{
827	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
828	  "name": "combined-example",
829	  "type": "registry:item",
830	  "dependencies": ["@tailwindcss/typography", "tw-animate-css"],
831	  "css": {
832	    "@import \"tailwindcss\"": {},
833	    "@import url(\"https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600&display=swap\")": {},
834	    "@plugin \"@tailwindcss/typography\"": {},
835	    "@plugin \"tw-animate-css\"": {},
836	    "@layer base": {
837	      "body": {
838	        "font-family": "Inter, sans-serif"
839	      }
840	    },
841	    "@utility content-auto": {
842	      "content-visibility": "auto"
843	    }
844	  }
845	}
846	```
847	
848	## Add custom animations
849	
850	Note: you need to define both `@keyframes` in css and `theme` in cssVars to use animations.
851	
852	```json title="example-component.json" showLineNumbers
853	{
854	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
855	  "name": "custom-component",
856	  "type": "registry:component",
857	  "cssVars": {
858	    "theme": {
859	      "--animate-wiggle": "wiggle 1s ease-in-out infinite"
860	    }
861	  },
862	  "css": {
863	    "@keyframes wiggle": {
864	      "0%, 100%": {
865	        "transform": "rotate(-3deg)"
866	      },
867	      "50%": {
868	        "transform": "rotate(3deg)"
869	      }
870	    }
871	  }
872	}
873	```
874	
875	## Add environment variables
876	
877	You can add environment variables using the `envVars` field.
878	
879	```json title="example-item.json" showLineNumbers {5-9}
880	{
881	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
882	  "name": "custom-item",
883	  "type": "registry:item",
884	  "envVars": {
885	    "NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL": "http://localhost:4000",
886	    "DATABASE_URL": "postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/postgres",
887	    "OPENAI_API_KEY": ""
888	  }
889	}
890	```
891	
892	Environment variables are added to the `.env.local` or `.env` file. Existing variables are not overwritten.
893	
894	<Callout>
895	
896	**IMPORTANT:** Use `envVars` to add development or example variables. Do NOT use it to add production variables.
897	
898	</Callout>
899	
900	## Universal Items
901	
902	As of `2.9.0`, you can create universal items that can be installed without framework detection or components.json.
903	
904	To make an item universal i.e framework agnostic, all the files in the item must have an explicit target.
905	
906	Here's an example of a registry item that installs custom Cursor rules for _python_:
907	
908	```json title=".cursor/rules/custom-python.mdc" showLineNumbers {9}
909	{
910	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
911	  "name": "python-rules",
912	  "type": "registry:item",
913	  "files": [
914	    {
915	      "path": "/path/to/your/registry/default/custom-python.mdc",
916	      "type": "registry:file",
917	      "target": "~/.cursor/rules/custom-python.mdc",
918	      "content": "..."
919	    }
920	  ]
921	}
922	```
923	
924	Here's another example for installing a custom ESLint config:
925	
926	```json title=".eslintrc.json" showLineNumbers {9}
927	{
928	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
929	  "name": "my-eslint-config",
930	  "type": "registry:item",
931	  "files": [
932	    {
933	      "path": "/path/to/your/registry/default/custom-eslint.json",
934	      "type": "registry:file",
935	      "target": "~/.eslintrc.json",
936	      "content": "..."
937	    }
938	  ]
939	}
940	```
941	
942	You can also have a universal item that installs multiple files:
943	
944	```json title="my-custom-starter-template.json" showLineNumbers {9}
945	{
946	  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
947	  "name": "my-custom-starter-template",
948	  "type": "registry:item",
949	  "dependencies": ["better-auth"],
950	  "files": [
951	    {
952	      "path": "/path/to/file-01.json",
953	      "type": "registry:file",
954	      "target": "~/file-01.json",
955	      "content": "..."
956	    },
957	    {
958	      "path": "/path/to/file-02.vue",
959	      "type": "registry:file",
960	      "target": "~/pages/file-02.vue",
961	      "content": "..."
962	    }
963	  ]
964	}
965	```
966

210	async function saveTransformCache() {
211	  if (!transformCacheDirty) {
212	    return
213	  }
214	
215	  await fs.mkdir(CACHE_ROOT, { recursive: true })
216	
217	  const payload = Object.fromEntries(
218	    Array.from(transformCacheManifest.entries()).sort(([a], [b]) =>
219	      a.localeCompare(b)
220	    )
221	  )
222	
223	  await fs.writeFile(
224	    TRANSFORM_CACHE_MANIFEST_PATH,
225	    JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)
226	  )
227	
228	  transformCacheDirty = false
229	}

231	async function getCachedStyledContent({
232	  styleName,
233	  baseName,
234	  filePath,
235	  source,
236	  styleHash,
237	  styleMap,
238	}: {
239	  styleName: string
240	  baseName: string
241	  filePath: string
242	  source: string
243	  styleHash: string
244	  styleMap: Record<string, string>
245	}) {
246	  const cacheKey = `${styleName}:${filePath}`
247	  const cachePath = path.join(TRANSFORM_CACHE_ROOT, styleName, filePath)
248	  const inputHash = hashContent(
249	    TRANSFORM_CACHE_VERSION,
250	    styleName,
251	    baseName,
252	    filePath,
253	    styleHash,
254	    source
255	  )
256	
257	  if (transformCacheManifest.get(cacheKey) === inputHash) {
258	    const cachedContent = await readFileIfExists(cachePath)
259	    if (cachedContent !== null) {
260	      return cachedContent
261	    }
262	  }
263	
264	  let transformedContent = await transformStyle(source, { styleMap })
265	  transformedContent = transformedContent.replace(
266	    new RegExp(`@/registry/bases/${baseName}/`, "g"),
267	    `@/registry/${styleName}/`
268	  )
269	  transformedContent = await formatGeneratedSource(
270	    transformedContent,
271	    path.join(getTemporaryRegistryRoot(styleName), filePath)
272	  )
273	
274	  await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(cachePath), { recursive: true })
275	  await fs.writeFile(cachePath, transformedContent)
276	
277	  if (transformCacheManifest.get(cacheKey) !== inputHash) {
278	    transformCacheManifest.set(cacheKey, inputHash)
279	    transformCacheDirty = true
280	  }
281	
282	  return transformedContent
283	}

285	async function runWithConcurrency<T, R>(
286	  items: T[],
287	  limit: number,
288	  worker: (item: T, index: number) => Promise<R>
289	) {
290	  const results = new Array<R>(items.length)
291	  let currentIndex = 0
292	
293	  await Promise.all(
294	    Array.from({ length: Math.min(limit, items.length) }, async () => {
295	      while (true) {
296	        const index = currentIndex++
297	        if (index >= items.length) {
298	          return
299	        }
300	
301	        results[index] = await worker(items[index], index)
302	      }
303	    })
304	  )

370	async function buildBasesIndex(bases: Base[]) {
371	  const registryImports = await Promise.all(
372	    bases.map(async (base) => {
373	      const { registry: importedRegistry } = await import(
374	        `../registry/bases/${base.name}/registry.ts`
375	      )
376	      return { base, importedRegistry }
377	    })
378	  )
379	
380	  let index = `// @ts-nocheck
381	// This file is autogenerated by scripts/build-registry.ts
382	// Do not edit this file directly.
383	import "server-only"
384	import * as React from "react"
385	
386	export const Index: Record<string, Record<string, any>> = {`
387	
388	  for (const { base, importedRegistry } of registryImports) {
389	    const parseResult = registrySchema.safeParse(importedRegistry)
390	    if (!parseResult.success) {
391	      console.error(`❌ Registry validation failed for ${base.name}:`)
392	      console.error(parseResult.error.format())
393	      throw new Error(`Invalid registry schema for ${base.name}`)
394	    }
395	
396	    const registry = parseResult.data
397	
398	    index += `
399	  "${base.name}": {`
400	
401	    for (const item of registry.items) {
402	      if (item.type === "registry:internal") {
403	        continue
404	      }
405	
406	      const files = normalizeRegistryFiles(item)
407	
408	      if (files.length === 0) {
409	        continue
410	      }
411	
412	      const componentPath = files[0]?.path
413	        ? `@/registry/bases/${base.name}/${stripFileExtension(files[0].path)}`
414	        : ""
415	
416	      index += `
417	    "${item.name}": {
418	      name: "${item.name}",
419	      title: "${item.title}",
420	      description: "${item.description ?? ""}",
421	      type: "${item.type}",
422	      registryDependencies: ${JSON.stringify(item.registryDependencies)},
423	      files: [${files.map((file) => {
424	        const filePath = `registry/bases/${base.name}/${file.path}`
425	        return `{
426	        path: "${filePath}",
427	        type: "${file.type}",
428	        target: "${file.target ?? ""}"
429	      }`
430	      })}],
431	      component: ${
432	        componentPath
433	          ? `React.lazy(async () => {
434	        const mod = await import("${componentPath}")
435	        const exportName = Object.keys(mod).find(key => typeof mod[key] === 'function' || typeof mod[key] === 'object') || "${item.name}"
436	        return { default: mod.default || mod[exportName] }
437	      })`
438	          : "null"
439	      },
440	      categories: ${JSON.stringify(item.categories)},
441	      meta: ${JSON.stringify(item.meta)},
442	    },`
443	    }
444	
445	    index += `
446	  },`
447	  }
448	
449	  index += `
450	}`
451	
452	  const outputPath = path.join(process.cwd(), "registry/bases/__index__.tsx")
453	  await writeIfChanged(
454	    outputPath,
455	    await formatGeneratedSource(index, outputPath)
456	  )
457	}
458	
459	async function buildBases(bases: Base[]) {
460	  const [baseImports, styleMaps] = await Promise.all([
461	    Promise.all(
462	      bases.map(async (base) => {
463	        const { registry: baseRegistry } = await import(
464	          `../registry/bases/${base.name}/registry.ts`
465	        )
466	        const result = registrySchema.safeParse(baseRegistry)
467	        if (!result.success) {
468	          console.error(`❌ Registry validation failed for ${base.name}:`)
469	          console.error(result.error.format())
470	          throw new Error(`Invalid registry schema for ${base.name}`)
471	        }
472	
473	        const registryItems = result.data.items.filter(
474	          (item) => item.type !== "registry:internal"
475	        )
476	
477	        const sourceFilePaths = Array.from(
478	          new Set(
479	            registryItems.flatMap((item) =>
480	              normalizeRegistryFiles(item).map((file) => file.path)
481	            )
482	          )
483	        )
484	
485	        const sourceFiles = new Map(
486	          await Promise.all(
487	            sourceFilePaths.map(
488	              async (filePath): Promise<readonly [string, string]> =>
489	                [
490	                  filePath,
491	                  await fs.readFile(
492	                    path.join(
493	                      process.cwd(),
494	                      `registry/bases/${base.name}/${filePath}`
495	                    ),
496	                    "utf8"
497	                  ),
498	                ] as const
499	            )

1	# Examples
2	
3	This directory contains demo components for the component documentation.
4	
5	## Directory Structure
6	
7	```
8	examples
9	├── base
10	│   ├── button-demo.tsx
11	│   └── ...
12	├── radix
13	│   └── ...
14	└── __index__.tsx    # Auto-generated by registry:build
15	```
16	
17	## Adding a New Example
18	
19	1. Create a new `.tsx` file in `examples/base` or `examples/radix`:
20	
21	```tsx
22	// examples/base/button-loading.tsx
23	import { Button } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/button"
24	
25	export function ButtonLoading() {
26	  return <Button disabled>Loading...</Button>
27	}
28	```
29	
30	2. Run the registry build to regenerate the index:
31	
32	```bash
33	pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build
34	```
35	
36	3. Use the example in documentation by referencing its name (filename without `.tsx`):
37	
38	```tsx
39	<ComponentPreview name="button-loading" />
40	```
41	
42	## Notes
43	
44	- Example files should be placed directly in `examples/base` or `examples/radix`, not in subdirectories.
45	- Base examples should import from `@/styles/base-nova/*`. Radix examples should import from `@/styles/radix-nova/*`.
46	- Both named exports and default exports are supported.
47	- After adding or removing examples, run `pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build` to update the index.

41	function Button({

45	  asChild = false,
46	  ...props

48	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {

62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document of my findings:

## Comprehensive Registry System Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the shadcn/ui v4 component library registry system. Here's what I found:

### **1. Registry Architecture Overview**

The registry system is multi-layered with clear separation:

**Core Structure:**
- **Source of Truth**: 
  - Components/UI live in: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base-name}/` (base, radix)
  - Examples live in: `/apps/v4/examples/{base-name}/`
- **Generated Indices**: 
  - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/__index__.tsx` - Generated base component index
  - `/apps/v4/registry/__index__.tsx` - Generated all styles component index
  - `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` - Generated examples index
- **Build Script**: `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` (orchestrates entire registry pipeline)

### **2. Registry Directory Structure**

For each base (e.g., `base`, `radix`):

```
registry/bases/{base-name}/
├── ui/                          # UI components (registry:ui type)
│   ├── button.tsx
│   ├── accordion.tsx
│   └── _registry.ts            # Registry definitions (manually maintained)
├── components/                  # Registry:component type
│   └── _registry.ts
├── examples/                    # Registry:example type
│   └── _registry.ts
├── blocks/                      # Registry:block type (complex components)
│   └── _registry.ts
├── hooks/                       # Registry:hook type
│   └── _registry.ts
├── lib/                         # Registry:lib type (utilities)
│   └── _registry.ts
├── internal/                    # Internal utilities
│   └── _registry.ts
└── registry.ts                  # Main registry file that aggregates all
```

### **3. Component Registration Format**

**In `_registry.ts` files (e.g., `ui/_registry.ts`):**

```typescript
import { type Registry } from "shadcn/schema"

export const ui: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "button",
    type: "registry:ui",
    files: [
      {
        path: "ui/button.tsx",
        type: "registry:ui",
      }
    ],
    registryDependencies: ["utils"],  // Other registry items this depends on
    meta: {
      links: {
        docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/base/button",
        examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/..."
      }
    }
  },
  // ... more items
]
```

**Key Properties for Registry Items:**
- `name`: Unique identifier for the component
- `type`: One of `registry:ui`, `registry:component`, `registry:block`, `registry:hook`, `registry:lib`, `registry:example`, etc.
- `files`: Array of files with `path` and `type`
- `registryDependencies`: Array of other registry items required
- `title`, `description`: Display metadata
- `dependencies`: NPM packages required
- `devDependencies`: Dev-only NPM packages
- `cssVars`: CSS variables to inject
- `css`: CSS rules to add
- `meta`: Custom metadata (links, category info, etc.)

### **4. Registry Aggregation in Main File**

In `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base-name}/registry.ts`:

```typescript
import { registryItemSchema, type Registry } from "shadcn/schema"
import { z } from "zod"

import { blocks } from "./blocks/_registry"
import { components } from "./components/_registry"
import { examples } from "./examples/_registry"
import { hooks } from "./hooks/_registry"
import { internal } from "./internal/_registry"
import { lib } from "./lib/_registry"
import { ui } from "./ui/_registry"

export const registry = {
  name: "shadcn/ui",
  homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com",
  items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse([
    ...ui,
    ...examples,
    ...lib,
    ...components,
    ...blocks,
    ...hooks,
    ...internal,
  ]),
} satisfies Registry
```

### **5. Example/Demo Registration - Separate System**

**Two Registry Systems:**

1. **Traditional Registry** (in `/registry/bases/{base}/examples/_registry.ts`):
   - Uses `registry:example` type
   - Maps to files in `/registry/bases/{base}/examples/`
   - Used for built-in component examples

2. **Live Examples Index** (auto-generated):
   - Reads from `/apps/v4/examples/{base}/` directly
   - Auto-generates `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx`
   - Each `.tsx` file becomes an example entry
   - Pattern: filename without `.tsx` extension becomes the example name

**Example Registration in Traditional Registry:**
```typescript
// In registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts
export const examples: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "button-example",
    title: "Button",
    type: "registry:example",
    registryDependencies: ["button", "example"],
    files: [
      {
        path: "examples/button-example.tsx",
        type: "registry:example",
      }
    ]
  },
  // ... more examples
]
```

**Live Example File:**
```typescript
// examples/base/dropdown-menu-radio-icons.tsx (auto-discovered)
"use client"

import { Button } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/button"
import { DropdownMenu, ... } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/dropdown-menu"

export function DropdownMenuRadioIcons() {
  // Component implementation
}
```

### **6. Build Script Pipeline**

The build process (`npm run registry:build`) executes in order:

1. **Build Bases Index** - Aggregates all base registries into `/registry/bases/__index__.tsx`
2. **Build Bases** - Generates styled versions of components
3. **Build Registry Index** - Creates `/registry/__index__.tsx` for all style combinations
4. **Build Examples Index** - Auto-scans `/examples/{base}/` and generates `/examples/__index__.tsx`
5. **Build Styles** - Generates style-specific registries for each style combination
6. **Build Blocks, Config, Colors** - Additional indices
7. **Copy UI to Styles** - Copies compiled components
8. **Build RTL** - Generates RTL versions for `base-nova` and `radix-nova`
9. **Cleanup** - Removes temporary files

### **7. Schema and Validation**

**Registry Item Schema** (`/apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json`):

Key validation rules:
- `name` and `type` are required
- `files` required, with each file having `path` and `type`
- `target` property only required for `registry:page` and `registry:file` types
- Supports `cssVars` with `theme`, `light`, `dark` variants
- Supports `css` object with nested rules
- Supports `registryDependencies` for registry items and npm `dependencies`

**Registry Schema** (`/apps/v4/public/schema/registry.json`):
- Wrapper containing `name`, `homepage`, and `items` array
- Items must be valid per registry-item.json schema

### **8. Generated Index Files**

**`/registry/bases/__index__.tsx`** (Auto-generated):
- Maps base names to indexed registry items
- Creates `React.lazy()` imports for components
- Includes dynamic export resolution
- Example structure:
```typescript
export const Index: Record<string, Record<string, any>> = {
  base: {
    button: {
      name: "button",
      type: "registry:ui",
      files: [...],
      component: React.lazy(async () => { ... }),
      ...
    },
    ...
  },
  radix: { ... }
}
```

**`/registry/__index__.tsx`** (Auto-generated):
- Contains same structure but for all style combinations
- e.g., `base-nova`, `radix-nova`, `new-york-v4`, etc.
- Used for runtime component lookup by the CLI

**`/examples/__index__.tsx`** (Auto-generated):
- Maps example names to lazy-loaded components
- Includes file paths for code display
- Example structure:
```typescript
export const ExamplesIndex: Record<string, Record<string, any>> = {
  base: {
    "dropdown-menu-radio-icons": {
      name: "dropdown-menu-radio-icons",
      filePath: "examples/base/dropdown-menu-radio-icons.tsx",
      component: React.lazy(async () => { ... })
    }
  }
}
```

### **9. Key Files to Modify/Create**

To register a new UI component:

1. **Create component file**: 
   - Path: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base-name}/ui/{component-name}.tsx`
   - File must export component (named or default)

2. **Add to registry definition**:
   - File: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base-name}/ui/_registry.ts`
   - Add entry to `ui` array with name, type, files, and metadata

3. **Create example file** (optional):
   - Path: `/apps/v4/examples/{base-name}/{component-name}-example.tsx` (optional)
   - OR: `/apps/v4/examples/{base-name}/{anything}.tsx` (auto-discovered)
   - File must export component

4. **Register example** (if using traditional registry):
   - File: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base-name}/examples/_registry.ts`
   - Add entry with `type: "registry:example"`

5. **Run build**:
   - Command: `pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build`
   - This regenerates all indices

### **10. Separate Registries**

The system supports multiple independent bases:
- **`base`** - Uses @base-ui/react
- **`radix`** - Uses @radix-ui/react
- Each gets its own namespace in indices
- Style combinations: `{base-name}-{style-name}` (e.g., `base-nova`, `radix-nova`)

### **11. Documentation Resources**

Key documentation files:
- `/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/registry-item-json.mdx` - Complete schema documentation
- `/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx` - Examples of different item types
- `/apps/v4/examples/README.md` - Quick guide for adding examples

---

### **Summary: What Files to Create/Modify**

To add a new component with demo:

```
✅ Create:
- /apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/ui/{component}.tsx

✅ Modify:
- /apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/ui/_registry.ts (add entry)

✅ Create (optional but recommended):
- /apps/v4/examples/{base}/{component}-demo.tsx (auto-discovered)

✅ Run:
- pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build (regenerates indices)
```

The build script handles:
- Index generation
- Lazy loading setup
- File path transformation
- Style combination generation
- CSS variable injection
- Export resolution

All done without manual index editing!

1	"use client"
2	
3	import * as React from "react"
4	import useEmblaCarousel, {
5	  type UseEmblaCarouselType,
6	} from "embla-carousel-react"
7	import { ArrowLeft, ArrowRight } from "lucide-react"
8	
9	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
10	import { Button } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button"
11	
12	type CarouselApi = UseEmblaCarouselType[1]
13	type UseCarouselParameters = Parameters<typeof useEmblaCarousel>
14	type CarouselOptions = UseCarouselParameters[0]
15	type CarouselPlugin = UseCarouselParameters[1]
16	
17	type CarouselProps = {
18	  opts?: CarouselOptions
19	  plugins?: CarouselPlugin
20	  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
21	  setApi?: (api: CarouselApi) => void
22	}
23	
24	type CarouselContextProps = {
25	  carouselRef: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[0]
26	  api: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[1]
27	  scrollPrev: () => void
28	  scrollNext: () => void
29	  canScrollPrev: boolean
30	  canScrollNext: boolean
31	} & CarouselProps
32	
33	const CarouselContext = React.createContext<CarouselContextProps | null>(null)
34	
35	function useCarousel() {
36	  const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext)
37	
38	  if (!context) {
39	    throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a <Carousel />")
40	  }
41	
42	  return context
43	}
44	
45	function Carousel({
46	  orientation = "horizontal",
47	  opts,
48	  setApi,
49	  plugins,
50	  className,
51	  children,
52	  ...props
53	}: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & CarouselProps) {
54	  const [carouselRef, api] = useEmblaCarousel(
55	    {
56	      ...opts,
57	      axis: orientation === "horizontal" ? "x" : "y",
58	    },
59	    plugins
60	  )
61	  const [canScrollPrev, setCanScrollPrev] = React.useState(false)
62	  const [canScrollNext, setCanScrollNext] = React.useState(false)
63	
64	  const onSelect = React.useCallback((api: CarouselApi) => {
65	    if (!api) return
66	    setCanScrollPrev(api.canScrollPrev())
67	    setCanScrollNext(api.canScrollNext())
68	  }, [])
69	
70	  const scrollPrev = React.useCallback(() => {
71	    api?.scrollPrev()
72	  }, [api])
73	
74	  const scrollNext = React.useCallback(() => {
75	    api?.scrollNext()
76	  }, [api])
77	
78	  const handleKeyDown = React.useCallback(
79	    (event: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLDivElement>) => {
80	      if (event.key === "ArrowLeft") {
81	        event.preventDefault()
82	        scrollPrev()
83	      } else if (event.key === "ArrowRight") {
84	        event.preventDefault()
85	        scrollNext()
86	      }
87	    },
88	    [scrollPrev, scrollNext]
89	  )
90	
91	  React.useEffect(() => {
92	    if (!api || !setApi) return
93	    setApi(api)
94	  }, [api, setApi])
95	
96	  React.useEffect(() => {
97	    if (!api) return
98	    onSelect(api)
99	    api.on("reInit", onSelect)
100	    api.on("select", onSelect)
101	
102	    return () => {
103	      api?.off("select", onSelect)
104	    }
105	  }, [api, onSelect])
106	
107	  return (
108	    <CarouselContext.Provider
109	      value={{
110	        carouselRef,
111	        api: api,
112	        opts,
113	        orientation:
114	          orientation || (opts?.axis === "y" ? "vertical" : "horizontal"),
115	        scrollPrev,
116	        scrollNext,
117	        canScrollPrev,
118	        canScrollNext,
119	      }}
120	    >

1	# Contributing
2	
3	Thanks for your interest in contributing to ui.shadcn.com. We're happy to have you here.
4	
5	Please take a moment to review this document before submitting your first pull request. We also strongly recommend that you check for open issues and pull requests to see if someone else is working on something similar.
6	
7	If you need any help, feel free to reach out to [@shadcn](https://twitter.com/shadcn).
8	
9	## About this repository
10	
11	This repository is a monorepo.
12	
13	- We use [pnpm](https://pnpm.io) and [`workspaces`](https://pnpm.io/workspaces) for development.
14	- We use [Turborepo](https://turbo.build/repo) as our build system.
15	- We use [changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for managing releases.
16	
17	## Structure
18	
19	This repository is structured as follows:
20	
21	```
22	apps
23	└── v4
24	    ├── app
25	    ├── components
26	    ├── content
27	    └── registry
28	        └── new-york-v4
29	            ├── example
30	            └── ui
31	packages
32	└── shadcn
33	```
34	
35	| Path                 | Description                              |
36	| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
37	| `apps/v4/app`        | The Next.js application for the website. |
38	| `apps/v4/components` | The React components for the website.    |
39	| `apps/v4/content`    | The content for the website.             |
40	| `apps/v4/registry`   | The registry for the components.         |
41	| `packages/shadcn`    | The `shadcn` package.                    |
42	
43	## Development
44	
45	### Fork this repo
46	
47	You can fork this repo by clicking the fork button in the top right corner of this page.
48	
49	### Clone on your local machine
50	
51	```bash
52	git clone https://github.com/your-username/ui.git
53	```
54	
55	### Navigate to project directory
56	
57	```bash
58	cd ui
59	```
60	
61	### Create a new Branch
62	
63	```bash
64	git checkout -b my-new-branch
65	```
66	
67	### Install dependencies
68	
69	```bash
70	pnpm install
71	```
72	
73	### Run a workspace
74	
75	You can use the `pnpm --filter=[WORKSPACE]` command to start the development process for a workspace.
76	
77	#### Examples
78	
79	1. To run the `ui.shadcn.com` website:
80	
81	```bash
82	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
83	```
84	
85	2. To run the `shadcn` package:
86	
87	```bash
88	pnpm --filter=shadcn dev
89	```
90	
91	## Running the CLI Locally
92	
93	To run the CLI locally, you can follow the workflow:
94	
95	1. Start by running the dev server:
96	
97	   ```bash
98	   pnpm dev
99	   ```
100	
101	2. In another terminal tab, test the CLI by running:
102	
103	   ```bash
104	   pnpm shadcn
105	   ```
106	
107	   To test the CLI in a specific app, use a command like:
108	
109	   ```bash
110	   pnpm shadcn <init | add | ...> -c ~/Desktop/my-app
111	   ```
112	
113	This workflow ensures that you are running the most recent version of the registry and testing the CLI properly in your local environment.
114	
115	## Documentation
116	
117	The documentation for this project is located in the `v4` workspace. You can run the documentation locally by running the following command:
118	
119	```bash
120	pnpm --filter=v4 dev
121	```
122	
123	Documentation is written using [MDX](https://mdxjs.com). You can find the documentation files in the `apps/v4/content/docs` directory.
124	
125	## Components
126	
127	We use a registry system for developing components. You can find the source code for the components under `apps/v4/registry`. The components are organized by styles.
128	
129	```bash
130	apps
131	└── v4
132	    └── registry
133	        └── new-york-v4
134	            ├── example
135	            └── ui
136	```
137	
138	When adding or modifying components, please ensure that:
139	
140	1. You make the changes for every style.
141	2. You update the documentation.
142	3. You run `pnpm registry:build` to update the registry.
143	
144	## Commit Convention
145	
146	Before you create a Pull Request, please check whether your commits comply with
147	the commit conventions used in this repository.
148	
149	When you create a commit we kindly ask you to follow the convention
150	`category(scope or module): message` in your commit message while using one of
151	the following categories:
152	
153	- `feat / feature`: all changes that introduce completely new code or new
154	  features
155	- `fix`: changes that fix a bug (ideally you will additionally reference an
156	  issue if present)
157	- `refactor`: any code related change that is not a fix nor a feature
158	- `docs`: changing existing or creating new documentation (i.e. README, docs for
159	  usage of a lib or cli usage)
160	- `build`: all changes regarding the build of the software, changes to
161	  dependencies or the addition of new dependencies
162	- `test`: all changes regarding tests (adding new tests or changing existing
163	  ones)
164	- `ci`: all changes regarding the configuration of continuous integration (i.e.
165	  github actions, ci system)
166	- `chore`: all changes to the repository that do not fit into any of the above
167	  categories
168	
169	  e.g. `feat(components): add new prop to the avatar component`
170	
171	If you are interested in the detailed specification you can visit
172	https://www.conventionalcommits.org/ or check out the
173	[Angular Commit Message Guidelines](https://github.com/angular/angular/blob/22b96b9/CONTRIBUTING.md#-commit-message-guidelines).
174	
175	## Requests for new components
176	
177	If you have a request for a new component, please open a discussion on GitHub. We'll be happy to help you out.
178	
179	## CLI
180	
181	The `shadcn` package is a CLI for adding components to your project. You can find the documentation for the CLI [here](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/cli).
182	
183	Any changes to the CLI should be made in the `packages/shadcn` directory. If you can, it would be great if you could add tests for your changes.
184	
185	## Testing
186	
187	Tests are written using [Vitest](https://vitest.dev). You can run all the tests from the root of the repository.
188	
189	```bash
190	pnpm test
191	```
192	
193	Please ensure that the tests are passing when submitting a pull request. If you're adding new features, please include tests.
194

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a thorough summary document for the user.

Based on my thorough exploration of the shadcn/ui component library, here's a comprehensive guide to how demo/example files work:

## Demo/Example File Organization

### 1. **Two Types of Example Files**

The library maintains two parallel example systems:

**A. Simple Demo Files** (`/apps/v4/examples/base/` and `/apps/v4/examples/radix/`)
- Located at: `/apps/v4/examples/base/*-demo.tsx`
- Used for quick, individual component demonstrations
- Examples: `button-demo.tsx`, `carousel-demo.tsx`, `accordion-demo.tsx`

**B. Comprehensive Example Files** (`/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/`)
- Located at: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/*-example.tsx`
- More detailed showcases with multiple use case variations
- Registered in the component registry system
- Examples: `accordion-example.tsx`, `tabs-example.tsx`, `button-group-example.tsx`

### 2. **Import Paths**

The import paths differ significantly between the two types:

**Simple Demos** import from styled paths:
```typescript
import { Button } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/button"
import { Card, CardContent } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/card"
```

**Registry Examples** import from registry paths:
```typescript
import {
  Accordion,
  AccordionContent,
  AccordionItem,
  AccordionTrigger,
} from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/accordion"
import { Button } from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/button"
```

The `@/styles/` path aliases point to pre-built styled versions, while `@/registry/` imports are the canonical source-of-truth for the component registry.

### 3. **Export Structure**

Both simple and registry examples use **default exports**:

```typescript
export default function AccordionDemo() { /* ... */ }
export default function TabsExample() { /* ... */ }
```

Named exports are NOT used for the main component.

### 4. **Structural Patterns for Compound Components**

**Simple Demo Pattern** (minimal):
```typescript
export default function CarouselDemo() {
  return (
    <Carousel className="w-full max-w-[12rem] sm:max-w-xs">
      {/* component content */}
    </Carousel>
  )
}
```

**Registry Example Pattern** (comprehensive, for compound components):
```typescript
import {
  Example,
  ExampleWrapper,
} from "@/registry/bases/base/components/example"

export default function TabsExample() {
  return (
    <ExampleWrapper>
      <TabsBasic />
      <TabsLine />
      <TabsVariantsComparison />
      <TabsDisabled />
      <TabsWithIcons />
      {/* ... more variations */}
    </ExampleWrapper>
  )
}

function TabsBasic() {
  return (
    <Example title="Basic">
      {/* single variation */}
    </Example>
  )
}

function TabsLine() {
  return (
    <Example title="Line">
      {/* another variation */}
    </Example>
  )
}
```

### 5. **Example and ExampleWrapper Components**

Located at: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/components/example.tsx`

**ExampleWrapper**:
- Container for all variations of a component
- Grid-based layout (2 columns on desktop, 1 column on smaller screens)
- Accepts custom className for layout control
- Example: `<ExampleWrapper className="w-full max-w-4xl lg:grid-cols-1">`

**Example**:
- Individual variation container
- Props: `title` (string), `className`, `containerClassName`
- The `title` prop displays as a label above the variation
- Creates a card-like container with padding and rounded corners
- Example: `<Example title="Basic">{children}</Example>`

### 6. **Registry Metadata Structure**

In `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts`:

```typescript
{
  name: "accordion-example",
  title: "Accordion",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["accordion", "button", "card", "example"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/accordion-example.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
},
```

**Key points**:
- `name`: kebab-case identifier (e.g., `"tabs-example"`)
- `title`: Display name for documentation
- `type`: Always `"registry:example"` for example files
- `registryDependencies`: Array of component names that this example depends on (these MUST be registered components)
- `files`: Array of file entries with path and type

### 7. **Compound Component Example: Tabs**

From `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/tabs-example.tsx`:

- **13 separate variations** of the tabs component
- Each variation is a helper function wrapped in `<Example title="...">`
- Main export uses `<ExampleWrapper>` to organize all variations
- Uses `IconPlaceholder` for cross-icon-library compatibility
- Includes style-specific Tailwind classes (e.g., `style-vega:rounded-lg`, `style-nova:rounded-lg`)
- Max-width constraints, padding/spacing specified per variation

### 8. **Special Annotations/Metadata**

**Style-aware Tailwind classes**:
```typescript
className="border style-vega:rounded-lg style-vega:p-6 style-nova:rounded-lg style-nova:p-4"
```
- Allows examples to look correct across different design styles (vega, nova, lyra, maia, mira, luma)

**containerClassName prop**:
```typescript
<Example title="Full Width" containerClassName="col-span-full">
```
- Used when a variation needs to span the full width of the grid

**IconPlaceholder component**:
- Allows specifying icons from different icon libraries
- Props: `lucide`, `tabler`, `hugeicons`, `phosphor`, `remixicon`

### 9. **File Naming Conventions**

- **Simple demos**: `{component-name}-demo.tsx` (e.g., `button-demo.tsx`)
- **Registry examples**: `{component-name}-example.tsx` (e.g., `button-example.tsx`)
- Both located in their respective directories in the registry structure

### 10. **For Creating a Stepper Example**

Based on this analysis, a Stepper example should follow this pattern:

**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx`

```typescript
import {
  Example,
  ExampleWrapper,
} from "@/registry/bases/base/components/example"
import { Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperContent } from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper"
import { Button } from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/button"

export default function StepperExample() {
  return (
    <ExampleWrapper>
      <StepperBasic />
      <StepperWithValidation />
      <StepperVertical />
      {/* ... other variations */}
    </ExampleWrapper>
  )
}

function StepperBasic() {
  return (
    <Example title="Basic">
      <Stepper>
        <StepperItem value="step-1">
          <StepperTrigger>Step 1</StepperTrigger>
          <StepperContent>Content for step 1</StepperContent>
        </StepperItem>
        {/* ... more steps */}
      </Stepper>
    </Example>
  )
}
```

**Registry entry** in `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts`:
```typescript
{
  name: "stepper-example",
